title: She is running Boston in honor of her mother who died of brain cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Madison Oakes, 23, from Boxford, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon in honor of her late mother.
Oakes credits the MGB cancer center for extending her mother's life after a Stage 4 brain cancer diagnosis.
Now an oncology nurse, Oakes participates in the marathon for Massachusetts General Hospital's team for the second year.

title: Boston police fatally shoot suspect allegedly wielding sword near Northeastern University campus
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Boston police fatally shot a man allegedly wielding a sword near Northeastern University on Hemenway Street.
The suspect, who was reportedly experiencing a mental health crisis, injured a police officer and an EMS clinician.
Police and Suffolk County District Attorney are investigating; the incident follows recent violent activity in the area.

title: She’s running Boston to honor her godmother who died of brain cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Abigail Dubois from Hudson, NH, runs the Boston Marathon to honor her late godmother, Diana, who died of brain cancer.
Dubois supports the Cancer Care Equity program at MGH, helping families with costs related to cancer treatments.
She qualified for Boston Marathon in 2025 and is running with the Massachusetts General Hospital team in April 2026.

title: 1 dead, 2 hospitalized in fatal Brockton house fire, officials say
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: A woman in her 40s died in a Brockton house fire that occurred early Saturday morning.
Two residents were hospitalized following the fire at 80 Glendale Ave, with intense flames engulfing the single-family home.
The cause of the fire is being investigated by Brockton Fire and Police, along with state police fire investigators.

title: NH suspect who allegedly shot at family members killed in officer-involved shooting
author: Darin Zullo
bullets: Matthew Masse, 38, was killed in a shootout with New Hampshire police after wounding an officer.
Masse allegedly fired a long gun at family members earlier on Saturday, prompting a police search.
Officers found Masse in woods near Ham Road, where an exchange of gunfire resulted in his death.

title: She’s running Boston with her best friend after both diagnosed with cancer
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Meg Gannon, from Canton, Mass., will run the Boston Marathon after a successful recovery from breast cancer.
Gannon's best friend, Peggy, also a cancer survivor, will join her in the marathon to support Mass General Hospital.
Both women aim to celebrate life and encourage others with their participation in the race on April 20, 2026.

title: ‘SNL’ recap: Best moments from Jack Black hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Jack Black hosted 'SNL' for the fifth time on April 4, with Jack White as musical guest.
Black joined the 'SNL' five-timers club alongside Jonah Hill, Tina Fey, Candice Bergen, and Melissa McCarthy.
Black performed an 'SNL'-version of 'Seven Nation Army' with White, featuring high-energy music and dancers.

title: Jaylen Brown opens up about past criticisms of his left hand: ‘There was some truth to it’
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Jaylen Brown acknowledged past criticism of his left hand due to injuries, affecting his performance until recent seasons.
Brown had major surgeries on his left wrist in 2021 and 2023, which affected his ability to dribble and drive left.
Brown has improved and is having a career-best season, averaging 28.7 points, 7.0 rebounds, and 5.3 assists per game.

title: ‘The Red Sox were my dream job’: Don Orsillo reflects on NESN tenure, reunites with former colleagues
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Don Orsillo returns to Boston to cover Padres-Red Sox games as their play-by-play announcer since 2016.
Orsillo, former NESN Red Sox voice until 2015, reflects on being remembered by fans and former colleagues.
Jim Rice and Tom Caron, NESN analysts, reunite with Orsillo during his visit to Fenway Park.

title: Alex Cora explains his decision to slot Roman Anthony at DH early this season
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Alex Cora explains using Roman Anthony as DH due to defensive shortcomings.
Roman Anthony showed offensive potential against the Padres, contributing a triple and a base hit.
Anthony still struggles defensively, conceding a key error in a 3-2 loss to San Diego.
